instructions.content: NEVER create an ADR without linking the source context file in the `context:` frontmatter field — decisions without provenance are untethered from their evidence.

NEVER edit or delete an accepted ADR — accepted ADRs are immutable records; only `status` and `superseded_by` may change, and superseding (not editing) replaces the decision. A proposed/unmerged ADR is still a draft and may be edited in place; immutability begins at acceptance.
ADR numbers are permanent identifiers — always increment to the next unused number, never reuse a number even if the old ADR was superseded.
Use `status: proposed` as the default on creation; promote to `accepted` after implementation starts.
NEVER skip creating an ADR when a .context/ file contains a clear decision — without an ADR the decision is invisible to future agents.

NEVER auto-flip an ADR's status from proposed to accepted, even when merge-status-sync.sh reports a strong (direct) link to a merged PR — acceptance is a deliberate decision requiring human confirmation, not a mechanical status field.
